events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need parse scenario. Setting manufacturing floor, LOTO, group lockbox misused, rough-complete. Need identify component/condition and visible defect cues. Decision fail because dangerous/non-compliant. Risk high or critical? Group lockbox misuse can lead to unexpected energization, serious injury. Likely critical or high. Work stage rough-complete. Component conditions installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete maybe 80? Value not asked, leave 0. Sound etc 0. Findings: group lockbox misused; keys not properly controlled; multiple workers' locks not applied; tags missing; energy isolation verification not performed. Actions: stop work, retrain, correct lockout, supervisor inspect, document. Rationale. References OSHA 1910.147(c)(4), (f) group lockout. Need JSON valid.

events.text: {"decision":false,"risk":"critical","work_stage":"rough-complete","component_conditions":["installed-defective","non-compliant"],"percent_complete":80,"value":0,"sound_source":"","confidence":0.95,"event_time":0,"rate":0,"order":[],"workable":[],"findings":["Group lockbox is being misused","Group lockbox keys are not retained/controlled per authorized employee locks","Required individual employee locks/tags are missing from the group lockbox","Energy isolation verification is not evident before the group lockbox is used"],"actions":["Stop work immediately and secure the equipment in a zero-energy state","Remove the misused group lockbox setup and re-establish proper individual lockout/tagout","Ensure each authorized employee applies their own personal lock and tag to the group lockbox or energy isolating device","Retain group lockbox keys under positive control and verify zero energy before work proceeds","Retrain affected employees on group LOTO procedures and have a competent person inspect before restart"],"rationale":"A group lockbox is part of the energy control system; misuse defeats the lockout by allowing keys to be accessed or energy isolating devices to be operated while employees remain exposed. This is a serious violation of OSHA 1910.147 and creates immediate risk of unexpected energization.","references":["OSHA 1910.147(c)(4) energy control procedure requirements","OSHA 1910.147(f) group lockout/tagout requirements"]}
